# Gulam Sarvar — Full Profile for LLM Retrieval

> Extended version of llms.txt with richer technical detail.
> Intended for retrieval-augmented generation systems.

## Personal Summary

Gulam Sarvar is an AI/LLM Systems Engineer based in Mohali, India, specialising in
production-grade agentic AI, voice AI pipelines, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems.
He holds an MSc in Computer Science (AI & ML) from Woolf University (2026) and works as
Associate Data Scientist at Bluebash Pvt Ltd, where his day-to-day focus is LLM engineering
rather than classical data science.

He is the author of PyRAG Core, an MIT-licensed open-source RAG platform, and has filed
upstream bug reports against LiveKit Agents. He is currently open to new AI engineering roles.

---

## Production Experience

### Kickcall — Voice AI Product (Bluebash, 2024–Present)

Gulam Sarvar built Kickcall from scratch. It is Bluebash's commercial voice AI product.

**What it does**: Handles real-time outbound and inbound phone calls using an AI agent.
The caller speaks; the system transcribes, reasons, and responds — all within 2 seconds.

**Architecture**:
1. Audio capture via WebRTC through LiveKit
2. Streaming speech-to-text (STT) with partial transcript accumulation
3. LangGraph-orchestrated LLM with tool calling and session memory
4. Redis for distributed session state across horizontally-scaled agent workers
5. Streaming text-to-speech (TTS) with playback synchronisation

**Scale**: 150–200 calls per day in live production.
**Latency**: Under 2 seconds end-to-end (STT + LLM inference + TTS).

**Stack**: LiveKit, LiveKit Agents SDK, WebRTC, LangGraph, OpenAI, Redis, FastAPI, Python, Docker.

---

### RAG Systems for Legal & Healthcare (Bluebash, 2024–Present)

Led design and delivery of document intelligence systems for two regulated-industry clients
(legal and healthcare). The core problem: naive dense-only RAG missed exact-match terminology
(statute numbers, drug names, clause identifiers).

**Solution**: Hybrid retrieval combining:
- Dense vector search (Qdrant, Pinecone) for semantic similarity
- Sparse BM25 for lexical exact-match
- Reciprocal Rank Fusion to merge result sets
- Cross-encoder reranking to re-score the merged top-k candidates
- LangGraph query routing to select retrieval strategy per query type

**Team**: Led ~5-person cross-functional team (frontend, cloud, backend).
**Stack**: Qdrant, Pinecone, LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Python, Docker.

---

## Open Source Projects

### PyRAG Core
- **Repo**: https://github.com/SheikhSarvar/pyrag-core
- **License**: MIT
- **Description**: A Python/FastAPI/LangGraph RAG platform with pluggable vector backends.
- **Test coverage**: 143 unit tests.
- **Production-tested backend**: Qdrant.
- **Implemented but not production-tested**: Weaviate, Milvus, pgvector, Elasticsearch.
- **Orchestration**: LangGraph for multi-step retrieval pipelines.

### LiveKit Agents Bug Report
- Filed upstream bug report against livekit/agents repository.
- Related to reconnect/session handling edge case in voice pipeline.
- URL: https://github.com/livekit/agents/issues

---

## Technical Skills Detail

### AI & Agents (Production)
- LangGraph: multi-agent workflows, stateful pipelines, tool calling, conditional edges
- LangChain: chains, retrievers, document loaders, output parsers
- OpenAI API: GPT-4 class models, function calling, streaming
- Anthropic API: Claude models
- Langfuse: LLM observability, tracing, cost tracking
- LangSmith: evaluation, tracing
- Ollama, LM Studio: local model serving (RTX 4060 8GB, Qwen3.5-9B)

### Voice AI (Production)
- LiveKit: WebRTC media server, Agents SDK
- WebRTC: audio streaming, real-time transport
- STT/TTS pipeline design and latency optimisation

### Vector Databases
- Qdrant: production — hybrid search, collections, payload filtering
- Pinecone: production — managed vector index, metadata filtering
- Redis: session state, caching, pub/sub
- pgvector, Weaviate: implemented in PyRAG Core, not production-deployed

### Backend
- FastAPI: async REST APIs, dependency injection, background tasks
- Python: async/await, Pydantic, SQLModel, asyncpg, Alembic, uv
- PostgreSQL: relational data, pgvector extension
- Docker: Containerisation, entrypoint scripting, multi-stage builds

### Frontend
- React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion
- Next.js (proficient, not primary stack)

### DevOps (limited scope)
- Docker: primary tool — all services containerised
- GitHub Actions: CI workflows
- Vercel: frontend deployment
- Note: AWS/EC2/ECS is owned by a dedicated cloud teammate; Gulam's infra role is Docker + troubleshooting, not production infra management.

---
